page: Google Cloud Platform c3-highmem-4 is a memory-optimized server instance featuring 4 vCPUs and 32 GB of RAM, yielding an 8 GB per core memory-to-vCPU ratio. Powered by an x86_64 Intel Xeon 8481C processor running at 2.7 GHz, the instance allocates 2 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. The processor includes a 105 MB L3 cache, 2 MB L2 cache, and 48 KB L1d cache. It supports advanced instruction sets such as Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions and Advanced Vector Extensions, alongside cryptographic acceleration via AES and SHA_NI.

Performance benchmarks indicate a divergence between single-core and multi-core capabilities. The instance achieves average performance in single-core stress-ng tests and Geekbench single-core evaluations. However, multi-core performance is relatively weak, as evidenced by its multi-core scores in Geekbench and stress-ng. Memory bandwidth is also limited, with uncached large-block read and write speeds falling into the lower performance tier.

In terms of application workloads, the c3-highmem-4 demonstrates average throughput for static web serving and single-threaded gzip compression. For lightweight machine learning tasks, it processes prompts and generates text at average speeds using a small 135M LLM. However, database operations and Redis SET performance are weak. This server is best suited for memory-intensive workloads that do not require heavy parallel CPU scaling, such as caching layers, moderate database tasks, or single-threaded data processing.
description: Google Cloud Platform c3-highmem-4 is a memory-optimized server configured with 4 vCPUs and 32 GB of RAM, providing a high memory-to-core ratio of 8 GB per vCPU. It is powered by a dedicated 2.7 GHz Intel Xeon 8481C processor with 2 physical cores, 2 threads per core, and a 105 MB L3 cache. The architecture supports Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions and AVX-512. Benchmark results show average single-core performance but weak multi-core scaling and memory bandwidth. The instance lacks local storage and bundled public IPv4 addresses. It is qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-heavy, single-threaded applications, moderate web serving, and lightweight LLM inference, but less suitable for highly parallelized compute tasks or intensive database operations.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c3-highmem-4 is a memory-optimized instance with 4 vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon 8481C CPU, offering solid single-core performance for memory-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c3-highmem-4 features 4 vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon CPU, optimized for memory-heavy, single-threaded workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual server featuring a high RAM-to-vCPU ratio and Intel Xeon processing for memory-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: 4 vCPUs and 32 GB RAM providing 8 GB of memory per core
Intel Xeon 8481C processor with AMX and AVX-512 instruction support
Average single-core CPU and text processing performance
No local NVMe storage or complimentary public IPv4 addresses
Best suited for memory-intensive, single-threaded applications and caching
